headline.text: L2 knife jam cost *30 minutes* at 02:10; L1 speed trial hit congestion and piece-count drops through the night.

per_plant_summary.L1: Sollich 3 ran caramel cluster all shift but fought printer scrap and dirty pouches from 16:00 onward. Sollich 2 raised line speed and immediately hit box congestion, piece counts falling to 17–19 (target 24–21), and two carts of check-weigher accumulation by 20:15. Tate's order is 5,400 of 14,000 cases; clean delayed, Monday trial pushed to 11:00.
per_plant_summary.L2: FFS knife stopped cutting pouches at 02:10, cleared in 30 minutes. ML2 and ML3 ran steadily; maintenance cleaned filters and lubed chains overnight. Grinder disassembled for weekend cleaning.
per_plant_summary.L3: ML4 delivered over 1 million bars on first shift despite two Theegarten stops (TG3 belt rip cleared by 08:40, TG4 chain cleaned by 10:12). Piece-count check at 12:28 found boxes 23–24 pieces short of 955 minimum. ML1 stopped three times for high temper, entered reheat at 07:03. MFM compensating for underweight pieces by dosing 33 per bag instead of 32; packaging defects totaled 21 lbs scrap across two pallets.

notes.text: Two chocolate barrels from L3 scrapped after scrap-barrel tags found; operator reminded to verify empties before filling.
First shift ML4 produced over 1 million bars; pizza party announced by Frederick Peairs.
Inventory adjustment processed for two MFM pallets (R221216, R227714) found in Staging but recorded as consumed; Fredy reminded team to enter all transactions promptly.

resolved.title: Sollich 3 C22 belt tracking, C25 infeed dead
resolved.summary: C22 conveyor not tracking properly, C25 belt not feeding product to MM4 at 15:37.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 16:11 — Caleb adjusted C22 tracking; C25 fixed by setting Weight Sensitivity from zero to 5000/3500, "it took off."

resolved.title: FFS knife jammed, not cutting pouches
resolved.summary: Knife stopped deploying after lunch break at 02:10; operator troubleshot, then maintenance pulled and cleaned knife assembly.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 02:38 — "Line is back up! approximately 30 minutes of downtime to take apart the knives and sealers, clean them and reassemble" (Zack Kurtz).

resolved.title: ML3 chain adjustment at start of shift
resolved.summary: Line stopped at 07:27 for chain tension work.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 08:01 — "The line up" (Molding Line 3 Pack).

resolved.title: TG4 down on caramel-caked chain
resolved.summary: Chain pulled at 08:58 with heavy caramel buildup; operators ran CIL during downtime, chain cleaned and reinstalled.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 10:12 — "It's running now, thanks Nelson" (Itzel Chavez). Preston noted chain was dry; maintenance to oil daily and pull weekly going forward.

resolved.title: TG3 belt ripped during CIL
resolved.summary: Belt tore at 08:12; Preston pulled it to the shop, replaced with slightly oversized spare that runs loose but functional.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 08:40 — "As of now it's working good" (Itzel Chavez). Belt is oversized and slightly loose; ops monitoring.

resolved.title: MFM badge cover broke, line stopped for search
resolved.summary: Employee badge cover snapped at 19:36; team halted to locate the fragment before resuming.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 19:52 — "We have located the badge" (Nathan Gevenois). Angel thanked Nathan and line team.

resolved.title: ML1 high temper stopping flow wrap
resolved.summary: Temper spiked above 1.0 at 05:19, line stopped, no flow wrap running.
resolved.resolution: Cleared at 05:40 — "Started running back up" (Raoul Lubain). Line stopped again at 06:30 and entered reheat at 07:03; Chad issued guidance to allow single temper <1.0 for ML1 continuous runs.

needs_attention.title: Sollich 2 speed trial causing box congestion and piece-count drop
needs_attention.summary: Line speed raised; box-belt congested, check weigher accumulated two carts of pouches, and piece count fell to 17–19 (target 24–21). Paused 20:15–20:45 to clear accumulation; x-ray bin filling fast as of 00:07.
needs_attention.status: Increased bark-breaker rotation to recover piece count. Paused 20:15–20:45; resumed but x-ray bin still filling fast at 00:07. 17-minute gap at 00:18 for lot change and accumulation run-down.

needs_attention.title: Sollich 3 printer scrap and dirty pouches from 16:00 onward
needs_attention.summary: MM3 printer head cleaned and ink changed at 17:13; alarm cleared but higher-than-normal pouch scrap continued through 18:03 due to dirty pouches and printing issues between 16:00–17:00.
needs_attention.status: Printer alarm cleared at 17:13; team monitoring. Higher scrap ongoing due to dirty pouches as of 18:03. Line lead asked maintenance to inspect and advise.

needs_attention.title: Tate's order 9,000 cases behind, delaying level 4 clean and Monday trial
needs_attention.summary: Order is 14,000 cases; only 5,400 complete as of 19:47. Finish is first priority per Chad, but will push level 4 clean and delay Monday MDLZ trial from 07:00 to 11:00.
needs_attention.status: Running 4 hours into dayshift 06/20 to finish order. Trial start moved to 11:00; MDLZ asked to arrive at 09:00 or later (Chad, 06:58).

needs_attention.title: MFM individual piece weights below 11g minimum, dosing 33 instead of 32
needs_attention.summary: Ray and Frederick sampled four bags; pieces ranged 10.35–14.35g (min spec 11g). Line now filling 33 pieces per bag to pass total-weight check instead of 32-piece spec.
needs_attention.status: Ray reduced target weight 5g at 10:50; rejects paused >5 minutes. Frederick coaching operators to inspect rejects and adjust. Line running 33-count as workaround as of 09:40.

needs_attention.title: MFM packaging defects: 21 lbs of open, doubled, and empty flow wraps
needs_attention.summary: Isis found 11 lbs of defective pouches on one pallet at 10:25, then another 10.56 lbs on a second pallet at 12:19.
needs_attention.status: Two pallets flagged and scrapped; no root cause or corrective action noted by window close.

needs_attention.title: ML4 piece count below 955 minimum in master-case check
needs_attention.summary: Mirella inspected two boxes at 12:28; Box 1 held 932 pieces (28.07 lb), Box 2 held 931 pieces (28.0 lb). Spec minimum is 955 per box.
needs_attention.status: Flagged at 12:28; no follow-up action or hold decision recorded in the window.

needs_attention.title: TG5 missing bolts on two of five rollers
needs_attention.summary: Kimberly reported during level 2 clean that two rollers are missing bolts entirely and one is very loose.
needs_attention.status: Flagged at 02:39; no repair logged by end of window.

needs_attention.title: TG3 missing scraper after level 2 clean
needs_attention.summary: Scraper not reinstalled on TG3 as of 06:40.
needs_attention.status: Kimberly flagged at 06:40; Chad asked Preston to follow up same day. No confirmation of install by window close.
